entry.title: SAP Stammtisch in Mannheim (1 of 2) - Let's chat Joule and all things SAP...
entry.content:
It's time to meetup in Mannheim!!!  While our SAP developer event is internal only, we are going to gather together with a relaxed Monday evening get-together and all are welcome! 🎊

📆When?
🕔 starting from 18:30 (6:30pm) on Monday, June 30

📍Where?
Augusta Restaurant Mannheim Germany 

🌤️ What to expect:
- A wonderful place for drinks or food
- chance to network and connect for those attending the SAP internal developer event and anyone else in the SAP ecosystem looking to drop in and say hi
- à la carte food & drinks 🍔🍷 (pay your own way)

🎟️ Who’s invited?
Everyone! Whether you’re an SAP employee attending the internal event or an SAP enthusiast - come hang out! 💬👋

Please hit the YES button so we know how many to expect.  See you soon!!!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch in Mannheim (2 of 2) Drinks only - Let's chat Joule and all things SAP...
entry.content:
It's time to meetup (again) in Mannheim!!!  While our SAP developer event is internal only, we are going to gather together with a relaxed Tuesday evening get-together for drinks and all are welcome! 🎊

📆When?
🕔After day 1 of of the internal event in Mannheim concludes before the second day - starting from 17:15 (7:15pm) on Tuesday, July 1st (calendar invite is not in 15 minute increments but reservation is for 7:15pm CET

📍Where?

Taproom Jungbusch


🌤️ What to expect:
- A wonderful place for drinks (no food offered at location so eat earlier 😉
- chance to network and connect for those attending our internal event and anyone else in the SAP ecosystem looking to drop in and say hi
- a wonderful local taproom🍺 (pay your own way)

🎟️ Who’s invited?
Everyone! Whether you’re an SAP employee attending the internal event or an SAP enthusiast - come hang out! 💬👋

Please hit the YES button so we know how many to expect.  See you soon!!!

entry.title: Re: Heartiest congratulations to our newest SAP Champions in July 2025!
entry.content: So happy for all the new SAP Champions and hope to see more and more activity for the benefit of all in the SAP Community.  Thank you all so very much for all you do for your community members!

entry.title: SAP CodeJam Roadshow LATAM - 2025, the First Half Recap, Being Present is About More Than Words.
entry.content:
Being present, is about more than words

So a few months ago back in April  I was chatting with my developer advocate colleague @ajmaradiaga about my new role as a Community Advocate and doing more outreach.  Attending more events in person specifically, and one type of event that we need to be present at is the SAP Inside Track (SIT's) and I was invited by the amazing team in Brazil that runs so many SIT events in Brazil. In particular I was asked to attend, and then asked to speak, and then speak twice, at the SIT in São Paulo.  The goal of this outreach was to have our team of Community Advocates be more present and listen and talk in person more, and not just online.  In talking about this plan with Developer Advocate Antonio Maradiaga he had a thought.   After the success of last years LATAM roadshow (that he and I did)  he said since he was going to do it again this year with a goal to visit a few cities they hadn't been to, Lima Peru and Mexico city, and also a repeat visit in Bogota.  But since I was going to be in São Paulo on a Saturday for the SIT and the encontro the day before (Antonio helped organize a local meetup called an encontro and @RobertaFernande picked a great spot) maybe I could host a #CodeJam there and then meet him and @RichHeilman the next week in Lima.  Of course I agreed and the plan was set to use the SAP office and have a GenAI CodeJam in São Paulo.

Being present, again and unintended effects:

Fast forward to SAP Sapphire in Orlando. I’m giving a presentation on community and also attending meetings with the SAP Mentors. In one meeting I’m next to @ChrisRae  and he says “mate, gotta head to an #Apphaus meeting, can you join?"

I say sure and follow him over.  Then when I walk in, I’m amazed to see a lot of faces in person that I’d been emailing with about growing the SAP Community presence at the Apphaus locations.  I say a few words and talk about how the team of Developer Advocates can host a CodeJam or I can have meetings at their space if they are willing to host.  All of the partners love this idea. Then a person came up at the end of the meeting named Renan Guedes. He said he runs the Apphaus in São Paulo and wanted me to visit when I was there. I said hey, why don’t I ask Antonio to move the CodeJam from our office to the Apphaus.

Apphaus meetinFirst time meeting Renan

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Pretty cool!  First half of the LATAM roadshow 2025 is set. 

  • CodeJam at the exec consulting Apphaus
  • Meetup at bar 
  • SIT São Paulo at the campus of PUC 
  • Then off to Lima for the next CodeJam and a quedada (meetup in español)
  • Bonus, I got a coffee with SAP Mentor @Raquel_PereiradaCunha me and Raquel

Being present, the reality

I land in Brazil. My colleague @SavioMarques  agrees to pick me up and we can go to get a coffee then attend get to the CodeJam and setup early. Savio showing me the best coffee!

It’s a great space and the team at Exed Consulting are so gracious. They welcome our local attendees and we are off showcasing how to sue GenAI in BTP. I have great conversations in English as my Portuguese is, lacking. We even did a podcast!

Apphaus group photo

CodeJam in action

Then after we have gone through our 5-6 hours of content we break for day. I’m hopeful to see a few of the attendees at the meetup along with some of the SIT attendees who are already in town.  I’m blown away. We get about 25 people. Several form the CodeJams and several sit.  A few SAP Champions are there like @Marssel700 and I even get a present for the next day, a couple of shirts, SIT shirts. One even has my name.

SIT Shirts

 

encontro São Paulo

I’m not too tired but want to make sure I’m able to get some rest and prepare for my two sessions and also have great conversations so I head back to the hotel, which for some reason has blue stains on the pillow case?

I called this section “being present, the reality” but I had no idea just how many great conversations I would have. How many photos I would take with the attendees. Roberta  even made a Brazilian flat Josh!!!

Brazilian flat Josh, also taller than me

 

I give my presentation on the technical side of the conference. I have a great lunch with a few locals and then my colleague Savio makes sure I meetup with a new colleague I met at the CodeJams named Arthur who agrees to make sure if I need anything he’s there to help.  Everyone is so kind.  For those interested in a photo but don't want a 1-1 we meet at the court for a group photo!

 

My second presentation is great and the audience is very happy when I end with a slide stating “Vai Conrinthians!” and the logo of the local favorite football team (soccer to me…), that was my loudest applause and cheers of the day!

Group selfie at SIT São Paulo

 

We have a wrap up meal with several of the SIT organizers and then the next I'm off to Lima Peru where there are two code jams and a meetup Called a quedada locally.  Me and the crew meet in the hotel lobby to strategize!

 

gotta be on the blue crue!

 The first of two CodeJam's is on ABAP so Rich takes the lead showing how to use Joule with ABAP code and prompting the system to give contextual responses based on the functions you put into your ABAP code.  The group in Lima is loving it and the exercises fly by as they consume code they know but in a new way.  ABAP CodeJam Lima

 

quedada Lima

I won’t be able to stay for the second CodeJam due to my travel schedule, but that’s ok cause I am sure the Lima attendees are in good hands with Antonio and Rich.  I even heard that most of the GenAI content on day 2 was translated on the fly to español by Antonio. 

My favorite memory of the CodeJam and quedada was what I usually say about every event, the people.  Conversations about music, food, life.  The amount of time some attendees took to travel to be with us is staggering and so overwhelming.  Being present really does something for me that I am not sure the attendees of our community event’s realize. It’s not about what we discuss, what they learn, what product issue we may solve.  It’s the connection, the memories made together that are chapters in this amazing virtual novel I'm writing, event by event, called “My SAP Community journey”.  I hope you can all be present with us in some way and write your own stories.  Obrigado, gracias and thank you!

Oh, I almost forgot, Antonio made some world tour shirts!

Roadshow tour shirts by Antonio

 roadshow shirt close up

 

Now, for the second half of the LATAM roadshow I will hand over to my colleague @ajmaradiaga to let us know how things go in Mexico City and Bogota!

p.s. I am so sorry to all I can't post all the selfie pics and your name here, but maybe you can post the one's you took as a reply???

entry.title: Story Time - The letters "D" and "K"
entry.content:
Hey, everybody. I'm here today in the Newtown Square Library, and I want to share something with you in story time mode. Today's story is going to focus on two letters, the letters D and K. Now, those letters might make you think of a band or maybe a fashion icon, but for me, they connect to something different.

Back in June, I was lucky enough to get to go to our SAP developer meeting in Dresden, Germany, and then I also went to the one in Mannheim. While I was in Dresden, I met someone who rocks blue hair like me. Her name is Doreen Kaestner. here is a photo of me and Doreen.

And she gave me something. This bracelet. I'm not a Swifty, but I have a friendship bracelet now from Doreen from Dresden, and it says SAP Dresden on it. And I've worn it ever since. The only time I take it off is when I touch up my beard, which I need to do this weekend. But I don't want to get blue hair dye all over the bracelet. So that's the only time I take it off. Otherwise, it stays on. And it's not just about Dresden and Doreen. It's a reminder of the entire SAP community, the family I'm proud to be a part of.

 

So fast forward a few weeks. I'm down in the LATAM Roadshow in South America. I do a few events in Brazil and Lima, Peru. And then I go home because it's time for vacation. And I found myself with my family in Stockholm, Sweden. Out of nowhere, in old city near the water, someone shouts, Hey, I know you! You're from SAP. And I was like, yeah. I shook his hand and I said, hey, what's your name? He said, I'm Dorian Kaucher, DK, another DK, just like Doreen. Dorian works in a small office in southern Germany called Markdorf and focuses on sustainability initiatives. And when I asked if I could take a picture with him, he said, well, I need one, too, because I want to show all my colleagues back in the office that I met you.

 Honestly it's it's crazy because you all make me so much better by being a part of this SAP community by showing up and by connecting but it's so flattering right the energy i get from being able to be recognized is awesome and i appreciate that but i wish i could remember all your names and all your initials when i meet you but these moments they matter they matter to me so know that i do keep them inside they really do matter so from Dresden to Stockholm June to August, all the way through the year, all the way through Latin America, all the time. I'm thinking about you and you give me so much energy. And I love being a part of this SAP community because of that and because of you. So I invite you, though, that if you need something, I can help you connect to other people. So for Dorian, if you want to connect with him and talk about sustainability initiatives and all the great work him and his team are doing down in Markdorf, let me know. And if you need help from Doreen with some development, she's over in Dresden and if she can't help you with her SAP skills, maybe somebody else on the team can, she'll help connect you. I love being a connector, and other people can then connect others from that. So it's a great thing being a part of this SAP community, online and in person.

And speaking of online, Devtoberfest starts the last week of September. So I hope you're registered, and we'll see you online for that for four weeks. And then on November 4th, we're going to be online and in person for SAP TechEd. We're going to be in Berlin November 4th through 6th as well as Louisville, KY the same dates with an on tour stop, and then some of us go the next week to Sydney, Australia and after that a couple weeks later we're in Bengaluru, India.

So I promise with all of this happening I'll have more stories for you very soon. Thank you for reading, bye.

entry.title: Re: Garden Update: the raised bed went wild
entry.content: Make sure to check out the SAP Developer News for August 28th...I think you will like!!!

entry.title: Re: Garden Update: the raised bed went wild
entry.content: You’re very welcome. Well deserved and I know @moshenaveh is happy to see such great human stories in our community.

entry.title: Re: The Unofficial SAP Community HTML Tags Documentation
entry.content:
Hi all of my fellow SAP Community members!  Always love your passion and commitment to raising the conversation up and being as constructive and helpful as possible with your post, and your comment replies for all members new & old to our online platform to see and learn from. 

To that end I wanted to thank Marian for this post as it gives me and the very hard working team behind the scenes a chance to engage and give you a short status update on this topic of HTML tags.  Please see the attached table below and know that we are working on an approach to enable additional tags.  - Josh

 

Current permissions:
 
HTML Tag
Allowed HTML Tag Attributes
Description
<a>
href, target
Creates a hyperlink
<b>
none
Bold
<blockquote>
dir
Creates an indented block of text
<br>
none
Line break
<center>
none
Centers text
<div>
align, class
Surrounds a block of content to be formatted together
<em>
none
Italicize
<font>
face, size, color
Controls font size, face and color
<hr>
none
Horizontal line
<i>
none
Italics
<li>
none
List items (used with <ol> or <ul>)
<ol>
 
Ordered list
<p>
align
A paragraph of text
<pre>
none
Pre-formatted text. Typically using a monospace font
<span>
class
Groups text to be formatted together
<strike>
none
Strikethrough
<strong>
none
Bold
<table>

cellspacing, cellpadding, border, width, height

Table
<td>
width, height
Table column
<tr>
width, height
Table row
<u>
none
Underline
<ul>
 
Unordered list

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Fun for Week 2: Petoberfest 🐱🐶🐵🐷🐻🐸🐔🐴🦬🦎🐠🦜🐧🐌🐛
entry.content: This is the cats that live in my house and AI making sure you see their real internal monologue, I do not claim them...what JJ really thinks the world is likeCricket hiding her fealingsCricket and some JJ's behind herJJ on the porch

entry.title: 9 Things You Need to Know About SAP Community at SAP TechEd in Berlin
entry.content:
9 Things You Need to Know About SAP Community at SAP TechEd in Berlin 

SAP TechEd in Berlin is just around the corner and it’s not your typical tech conference: it’s where SAP Community comes home. To help you make the most of your time on-site, here are 9 must-know ways to experience the community in action. 

1. Connect at the Community Clubhouse 

Think of this as your community hub. It’s the place to meet other members alongside the SAP Community team, swap ideas, share experiences, and just hang out with people who are as passionate about SAP as you are.  Come join your community in Hall 1.2  

2. Dive into the Developer Garage 

The Developer Garage is a hands-on playground where you can work alongside Developer Advocates on real projects. With 7 tracks and 32 tutorials, it’s the perfect space to get practical experience and try out the latest SAP technologies. 

3. Learn from the Experts 

From product owners in AI, ABAP, and CAP to SAP Champions and Mentors, the “Meet the Experts” tables are where you can ask questions, get insights, and gain practical tips straight from the people building SAP solutions. 

4. Explore the Community Theatre 

Catch 33 community talks, participate in trivia competitions, and check out the DemoJam at the end of Day 1 (15:00–16:15 Berlin time, session DJ1295), hosted by Mr. Bluebeard himself. Teams will have 6 minutes to tackle a real business challenge using SAP BTP, showcasing innovation, creativity, and fun. Stop by the Community Clubhouse to see who takes home the DemoJam trophy.  

5. Explore Learning and Certification Opportunities 

SAP TechEd in Berlin offers a dedicated space for certifications. Learn what certifications are available, understand their value, and even take free onsite certification exams for SAP Generative AI Developer and SAP Build Developer in Berlin, Bangalore, or Louisville. Find more details and register for the exam. 

6. Step into the SAP TechEd Experience Center 

Discover the future of enterprise technology, from Business AI to live robotic demos. Experience gamified learning, explore customer use cases, and take home a smart tag and learning badge to continue your journey long after the event.  Make your way to Hall 3.2 for all the experience center has to offer. 

7. Get to Know the SAP Community Platform 

Whether you’re new or seasoned, the SAP Community info space will help you navigate the platform, find groups, and even learn how to become an event organizer or start a local location group. 

8. Connect with Mentors and Champions 

Curious about the SAP Mentors or SAP Champions programs? Stop by booth 103 in the Clubhouse, learn about the programs, and discover how you can become more involved in the community. 

9. Don’t Miss the Developer Keynote 

The developer keynote is a highlight for anyone interested in the latest innovations and the roadmap of SAP technologies. 

SAP TechEd in Berlin isn’t just about sessions and workshops, it’s about being part of the SAP Community in real life. Whether you’re rolling up your sleeves in a hands-on tutorial, swapping stories at a casual meetup, or catching insights from an expert talk, every corner of the event is designed to help you connect, share, and grow alongside fellow community members. 

We can’t wait to see you there! Come ready to learn, share, and be part of the SAP Community energy 🤝

entry.title: SAP Meetup Singapore!!!
entry.content:
It's time to meetup in Singapore!!!  When our SAP developer CodeJam ends, the fun does not.  Come over and join us for a networking get together at Harry's Mapletree - (Business City).   SAP Community members and those just SAP-curious are encouraged to gather together in this relaxed Thursday evening meetup and all are welcome! 🎊


📆 When?
🕔 starting from 17:30 (5:30pm) on Thursday, November 20

📍 Where?
Harry's Mapletree Business City
40 Pasir Panjang Rd, #02-43/44 Mapletree Business City II, Singapore 117383


🌤️  What to expect:
- A wonderful place for drinks or food
- chance to network and connect for those presenting and attending the SAP developer CodeJam and anyone else in the SAP ecosystem looking to drop in and say hi
- à la carte food & drinks 🍔🍷 (pay your own way)

🎟️  Who’s invited?
Everyone! Whether you’re an SAP employee attending the CodeJam event or an SAP enthusiast - come hang out! 💬👋

Please hit the YES button so we know how many to expect.  See you soon!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2025 - Nov 14th
entry.content: WIsh I could be there

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne November '25
entry.content: Can’t wait to see everyone there!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition 🇧🇷 (Kicking off in March and continuing in April)
entry.content: Guess what, from Curitiba to Recife your Barba Azul will be there!!!! Can’t wait to see you all.

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Lisbon
entry.content:
SAP Community Lisbon is getting together around the Portugal SAP Connect Day.
Join us to connect, share experiences, and discuss what’s shaping the SAP ecosystem in Portugal.
 
A SAP Community Lisboa junta-se para celebrar o Portugal SAP Connect Day.
Um momento para partilhar experiências e debater o futuro do ecossistema SAP em Portugal.
 
Time/Date
16th of April, from 18:30 until 20:00
Venue:
Pão da Vila República 
Av. República 63 B, 1050-189 Lisboa, Portugal

entry.title: A recap of my amazing 4 stops in Brazil for the 3rd annual LATAM Roadshow 2026
entry.content:
As you may have seen we had an amazing 3rd SAP CodeJam LATAM roadshow organized for the third year by @ajmaradiaga and while I couldn't be there in March I made it down for not only some CodeJams but also an SAP Inside Track.  I got to meet with some amazing SAP Community Members, a few of our SAP Champions and some of my Community Relations colleagues.  Here is my stop by stop recap.  

Day 1 in #Brazil of my third annual LATAM roadshow for is completed (not counting travel days 😜 ) where I got to see some of the amazing sites in #Curitiba before heading to a meetup with a few colleagues, organizers, and an amazing sponsor: CHPro


The AI Core CodeJam with @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman  was sponsored by the amazing team at CHPro Solutions led by Calixto Herkert and our location host university Escola Politécnica PUCPR.

The pictures can’t capture the amazing energy and love from all the participants and their enjoyment going though the #CodeJam !
Many thanks to the volunteer photographer (I included some of her pics, abrigado!)

The organizers of the SAP Community Curitiba deserve all the credit, from @SimoneAzevedo   to @LuizSouza_cwb   and @AdrianoCWB .  Such a well organized event. I can’t wait to be back in Curitiba but now I’m off to #Campinas for an SAP Inside Track. SAP Community  is strong!

____

Stop number two of the LATAM roadshow for me was an amazing trip to a Campinas, a city just north to the north of São Paulo. The event is an SAP inside track (SAP Inside Track Campinas Inside Track Brasil 🇧🇷 just outside of Sao Paulo ) and I get to connect with my colleagues @antonia_zorn @noravonthenen and @Vitaliy-R  , as well as continue with @RichHeilman and @thomas_jung , but unfortunately for Rich and Thimas, this will be their last stop of the tour.

 

Also exciting is getting to meet so many community members, especially our SAP champions @RobertaFernande  , @MRobalinho , and @Marssel700 . Truly, there were so many volunteers and an amazing campus that hosted our event lots of great sponsors and familiar faces form the last two years. Kicking off today with a co-presentation with Antonia was fun and then watching my colleagues present, as well as so many other customers and partners from the SAP community. It was so many people learning and sharing about SAP  in the Brazil community, it’s just something that I cannot express enough, how vibrant the Volunteer SAP Community  is and the interest of the attendees. The food was amazing. The capybara‘s were amazing and I appreciated every conversation and photo, but I’m sure you could tell that….


Next, we got together for a meet up at an amazing local brewery. I don’t even think there was a way to count the number of people who came over and enjoyed time together socializing.


But like all good things this stop had to come to an end so me, Nora, Vitaliy and Antonia packed up and are off to our next stop Porto Alegre. See you there!
_____

My 3rd stop of the LATAM CodeJam tour was continuing with Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  , Nora von Thenen  and Antonia Zorn  to São Leopoldo/ Porto Alegre. This southern Brazil city and its people are amazing but wow the SAP Labs office is sooooo amazing.
 

 


We had two CodeJams and a meetup between Monday/Tuesday. A packed house both days. And a special shoutout to our local support who’ve been with me in Curitiba and will also join at my last sip of Recife. Vitor Ugo Fiori  Vitória Cristofolli.

 

 

 

 
The CodeJams were amazing and the attendees so great at participating and engaging both days. It was also great to attend a meetup at a local German themed fun event center with billiards and bowling. And I got to eat my first Xis thanks to Vitor’s guidance. I also got to trip in dramatic fashion in Parque Moinhos de Vento while out for a jog with Vitaliy, and thank goodness he was right there to pick me up as any good Developer Advocate would do!

 

 


On a side note, I also was lucky enough to meet some local SAP colleagues who before now I’ve only seen online. Thank you for your time and I wish we had more chances to meet as we are all SAP family.

 

p.s. When Antonia says “Did you see the colleague in the next room with blue hair?” Well I think you know what happened next, right Ângela Schmidt

__

And stop 4 for me of the #LATAM  #Brazil roadshow is in the books. It’s actually the end of the whole tour for the team in Brazil which is sad but was such a great time for the SAP Community . A special shoutout to Vitória Cristofolli  and Vitor Ugo Fiori  for their support.

Arriving from Porto Alegre to #Recife was such a dramatic change. A northern coastal town with such a different look and feel to the architecture and of course the ocean views

I did a quick tour of the downtown and actually got to see the first synagogue ever built in the Americas (Synagoga Kahal Zur Israel) which showed me so much personal history I never thought I’d experience and will share with my Rabbi.



Then it was back to work. Me, Nora von Thenen  , Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  and Antonia Zorn  were hosted by the amazing team at Accenture led by João Otto Gethmann Jr  .


The attendees were so warm and attentive and enjoyed learning from Witalij on day 1 and Nora on day 2. Me and Antonia shared about the community and also had the pleasure of seeing so many locals at our meetup on a rooftop.

Stories were shared, local food and drink consumed and code discussions all around.  SAP Community, #barbaAzul shall return…

entry.title: Re: A recap of my amazing 4 stops in Brazil for the 3rd annual LATAM Roadshow 2026
entry.content: You are so kind @Marssel700 and we were so lucky to get to visit and see you!  To hear feedback like this is so hearwarming.  Abrigado

entry.title: My weekend event where I didn't think about work...
entry.content:
While this isn't enough for a hobbies series post I did want to showcase for those not in the Philadelphia, PA USA area the amazing local run that I get to participate in that has 40,000 runners.  My mile time is getting slower so I need to be better about training, but hey, I finished!  This was my 6th Broad Street 10 Miler in a row last 6 years.  I started jogging more when the pandemic shut down our exercise facilities.  I did a few half marathons but that caused more injury than healthy side effects.  Maybe when I am lighter a few pounds/kilos I will attempt longer than 10 miles/18KM.   Feel free to comment here with your local runs or other events you love.

Here are a few pics from this year, along with my twin wearing the last 6 finisher medals.  

Leaving the train that rides 10 miles north

 

racers in front of me

entry.title: Code Connect unofficial prelude meetup/Stammtisch
entry.content:
As my good friend @vobu has posted on his LinkedIn,  it's time again for a CodeConnect and we invite the entire local sap community in and around Walldorf / St. Leon-Rot  to the Unofficial Official Prelude event.

We will be meeting at "broom-pub" (Besenwirtschaft) Ihle: Höfe am Sträßel 3, 69231 Rauenberg
Map to ihle Besen
🕔 Monday, July 13, from 17:00 CEST on till...
🏎️ just 10 min by car from ROT03 (or just the right sobering walk time 😸 )
🥨 appetizers provided by the code connect host, then self-pay
↪️ just show up, no sign-up required
🕺 dress code: business casual ... not.   Ha! Let's just mingle and be a merry band for the night 🤙

Hope to see you there!

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2026: Registration is live!
entry.content:
SAP TechEd’s 30th anniversary!

Can you believe that SAP TechEd turns thirty this year?  Let’s party like it’s 1996 (portable CD Discman anyone?!)  In honor of all the great TechEd moments, we’ll take a trip down memory lane with you, celebrating TechEd’s legacy as the premier technical event for developers in the past, the present, and especially into the future.

We’ll be back in Berlin this October with the full-fledged, in-person, old school, TechEd experience you know and love. So, what’s in the works?  You name it: Hands-on Workshops, Strategy Talks, Road Maps, Deep Dives, Demo Stations and my favorite, a full-blown Community Clubhouse with fan favorites like the Developer Garage and the Community Theater. Berlin is your place to connect with SAP experts, Developer Advocates and your TechEd friends and neighbors in the flesh! TechEd fans rejoice!

What if I’m looking for more of a “sweatpants and sofa” vibe?

Fear not, my friend!  Throughout the three days, we'll be livestreaming sessions from Berlin for our live virtual show.  Many of our in-person Berlin sessions will be streamed live and available on-demand, and we’ll also have a virtual Q&A experience, where experts field questions from both live and virtual audiences.  In fact, much of our virtual show will take place from our virtual studio right on the Berlin show floor, with exclusive segments designed for our virtual audience.  

What kinds of sessions can I expect?

Great question.  We'll have different session formats so you can choose how you prefer to learn. Want to sit back and hear all about SAP strategy from our experts?  Or are you looking to get your hands dirty with some coding?  Are you looking for a high-level overview or want to dive deep into a particular topic? The choice is totally yours.

As we all know, at the end of the day, SAP TechEd is about community above all else, and we’ve got so much planned to bring us together in Berlin:

Come hang with us!

It's all about connecting, learning, and celebrating our community. It’s SAP TechEd and we’re beyond pumped and honored to bring it all to you this October! Register today and let me know what you are looking forward to the most.
